sub_problems: How can a single low-bandwidth internal probe (one residual-stream layer, one logit fingerprint) be read as a structured discrete taxonomy of qualitative states rather than as a single continuous scalar?
What is the right vocabulary size and granularity for such a taxonomy — too few categories lose the resolution operators need; too many become hard to recognize reliably from one probe?
How should the taxonomy be calibrated and re-calibrated across model versions so that the same label names continue to mean the same operational state?
How can downstream automation consume taxonomy labels reliably (act on 'wiry' state differently from 'slippery' state) without dropping back to brittle threshold rules on a scalar?
